frontmatter.title: Tool #01 Quick Reference — ABA Feasibility & Necessity Assessment

frontmatter.authors_or_orgs: John Twigg / DFID Disaster Risk Reduction Interagency Coordination Group
frontmatter.relevance: Provides a comprehensive framework of community resilience characteristics used to design community-based DRR activities, engagement platforms, and MEL indicator sets in urban response contexts.
frontmatter.title: Characteristics of a Disaster-Resilient Community: A Guidance Note

frontmatter.relevance: The foundational IASC inter-agency strategy for urban humanitarian response, establishing coordination principles and priorities that underpin area-based approaches and cluster-municipal alignment.
frontmatter.title: IASC Strategy: Meeting Humanitarian Challenges in Urban Areas

frontmatter.original_filename: Building Urban Resilience Principles, Tools, and Practice.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: Provides risk-based city and neighborhood screening tools, city resilience investment logic, and municipal integration frameworks applicable to DRR-informed area selection and transition planning.
frontmatter.title: Building Urban Resilience: Principles, Tools, and Practice

frontmatter.authors_or_orgs: Elizabeth Parker and Victoria Maynard / IIED
frontmatter.original_filename: Humanitarian response to urban crises a review of area-based approaches (2).pdf
frontmatter.relevance: Foundational review that defines what constitutes a genuine area-based approach — geographically targeted, multi-sectoral, and participatory — and distinguishes it from merely location-based programming.
frontmatter.title: Humanitarian Response to Urban Crises: A Review of Area-Based Approaches
used_for: Urban ABA Definition and Design Logic

frontmatter.authors_or_orgs: UNDRR (United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction)
frontmatter.original_filename: Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 20152030.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: The global policy framework for DRR that defines the four priorities for action — understanding risk, risk governance, investment in resilience, and preparedness — used to align urban ABA programming with DRR strategy and Build Back Better principles.
frontmatter.title: Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 2015–2030

frontmatter.original_filename: Stepping Back Understanding Cities and Their Systems.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: Provides the conceptual foundation for urban systems thinking in humanitarian response, explaining how cities function as interconnected systems of infrastructure, markets, governance, land, services, and social networks — essential for ABA appropriateness decisions and area diagnosis.
frontmatter.title: Stepping Back: Understanding Cities and Their Systems

frontmatter.authors_or_orgs: IFRC (International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies)
frontmatter.relevance: Provides guidance for Red Cross/Red Crescent engagement in urban resilience programming, including urban resilience action design principles applicable to integrated area strategy development and technical design.
frontmatter.title: Building Urban Resilience: A Guide for Red Cross and Red Crescent Engagement and Contribution

frontmatter.relevance: One of the most operationally detailed urban analysis toolkits, covering stakeholder and power mapping, service gap analysis, market and service provider mapping, and referral pathway design — core instruments for area profile and systems diagnosis.
frontmatter.title: Urban Context Analysis Toolkit: Guidance Note for Humanitarian Practitioners
used_for: Local Market and Service Provider Mapping

frontmatter.authors_or_orgs: Lucy Mohiddin, Gabrielle Smith, and Laura Phelps / IIED
frontmatter.original_filename: Smith and Phelps, Urban Response Analysis Framework.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: Provides the Urban Response Analysis Framework (URAF) — the primary tool for designing integrated multi-sector responses and comparing response options (cash, in-kind, direct works, referral, advocacy, technical assistance) in urban contexts.
frontmatter.title: Urban Response Analysis Framework: Guidance Note for Humanitarian Practitioners

frontmatter.authors_or_orgs: John Sanderson and Naoko Sitko / IIED
frontmatter.original_filename: Urban area-based approaches in post-disaster contexts (2).pdf
frontmatter.relevance: The most comprehensive operational guide for urban area-based approaches in post-disaster contexts, covering suitability assessment, area selection, participatory planning, needs prioritization, implementation sequencing, MEL, and handover — spanning all major lifecycle stages.
frontmatter.title: Urban Area-Based Approaches in Post-Disaster Contexts

frontmatter.original_filename: How to Make Cities More Resilient.pdf
primary_topics: Ten Essentials for Making Cities Resilient
frontmatter.relevance: Provides the Ten Essentials for Making Cities Resilient framework, which is used for municipal DRR diagnostic assessments and technical design checklists in area-based programs targeting municipal resilience goals.
frontmatter.title: How to Make Cities More Resilient: A Handbook for Local Government Leaders (2017 Edition)

frontmatter.relevance: Case study documenting PCI's neighbourhood-based approach in Guatemala City and Port-au-Prince, providing empirical evidence for area-based approaches in informal urban settlements in Latin America and the Caribbean.
frontmatter.title: Barrio Mio and Katye: PCI's Neighbourhood Approach in Cities
used_for: Reference case study for ABA design and validation

frontmatter.original_filename: Urban ABA guidance; IRC Urban Context Analysis Toolkit; ALNAP GPR 12.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: The most comprehensive global review of urban humanitarian response practice, covering protection risks, sector design, implementation sequencing, market-based programming, and outcome harvesting — a primary reference for multiple tools across lifecycle stages.
frontmatter.title: ALNAP Global Practice Review 12: Urban Humanitarian Response
used_for: Local Market and Service Provider Mapping

frontmatter.authors_or_orgs: Global Shelter Cluster / Urban Settlements Working Group
frontmatter.original_filename: Area-Based Approaches in Urban Settings Compendium of Case Studies.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: Multi-case compendium of area-based approach implementations across urban settings, providing evidence for settlement delineation, area strategy development, intervention package design, outcome documentation, and scale-up learning.
frontmatter.title: Area-Based Approaches in Urban Settings: Compendium of Case Studies

frontmatter.relevance: The most operationally detailed guide for conducting area-based assessments using key informant interviews, covering sampling methodology, questionnaire design, data quality, and analysis — the primary methodological reference for area profile and reassessment field work.
frontmatter.title: Area-Based Assessment with Key Informants: A Practical Guide

frontmatter.original_filename: Meeting Humanitarian Challenges in Urban Areas.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: The 2020 update to the IASC urban humanitarian strategy — pending extraction to determine updated coordination principles and guidance relevant to area-based approaches.
frontmatter.title: IASC Strategy: Meeting Humanitarian Challenges in Urban Areas (2020 Update)
used_for: Reference for updated IASC urban coordination guidance

frontmatter.original_filename: Standard Terms of Reference for Area-Based Coordination.pdf
frontmatter.relevance: The most recent IASC standard defining terms of reference for area-based coordination mechanisms — the authoritative reference for designing coordination structures in urban ABA programs.
frontmatter.title: Standard Terms of Reference for Area-Based Coordination

frontmatter.original_filename: Urban multi-sector vulnerability assessment tool for displacement contexts (UMVAT).pdf
frontmatter.relevance: Provides a multi-sector vulnerability assessment tool specifically designed for urban displacement contexts, applicable to HEVC mapping and area profile development in crisis settings with significant IDP populations.
frontmatter.title: Urban Multi-Sector Vulnerability Assessment Tool (UMVAT): Guidance Note for Displacement Contexts
used_for: Area profile development in displacement contexts
